message:
Tenemos una API en TypeScript que procesa 500k requests/día
con picos de hasta 5000 rps. La latencia p99 actual es de
305ms y queremos llevarla a menos de 86ms.
Stack actual:
- App Service con 6 instancias
- MongoDB sin read replicas
- Sin caché
- Grafana para monitoreo
¿Dónde debería empezar a optimizar? ¿Vale la pena migrar a Azure Functions
para aprovechar el autoscaling? ¿Qué patrones de caché me recomiendas?
¿Qué herramienta uso para detectar misconfiguraciones en Terraform?
¿Qué es un Ingress Controller y cuáles son los más usados?

Tengo el siguiente manifiesto de Kubernetes para el servicio api-gateway y estoy
viendo CrashLoopBackOff en producción. Estos son los últimos logs:

```
[2026-04-23T04:56:41Z] CrashLoopBackOff: back-off 4m restarting failed container
[2026-04-23T04:56:41Z] Error from server (Timeout): etcd leader changed
[2026-04-23T04:56:41Z] OOMKilled: container api-gateway exceeded memory limit 1159Mi
[2026-04-23T04:56:41Z] 0/10 nodes are available: 4 Insufficient cpu, 2 Insufficient memory
[2026-04-23T04:56:41Z] Error from server (Timeout): etcd leader changed
[2026-04-23T04:56:41Z] Error from server (Timeout): etcd leader changed
[2026-04-23T04:56:41Z] OOMKilled: container api-gateway exceeded memory limit 1159Mi
[2026-04-23T04:56:41Z] Error from server (Timeout): etcd leader changed
[2026-04-23T04:56:41Z] 0/10 nodes are available: 4 Insufficient cpu, 2 Insufficient memory
[2026-04-23T04:56:41Z] 0/10 nodes are available: 4 Insufficient cpu, 2 Insufficient memory
[2026-04-23T04:56:41Z] CrashLoopBackOff: back-off 4m restarting failed container
[2026-04-23T04:56:41Z] OOMKilled: container api-gateway exceeded memory limit 1159Mi
```

Y este es el deployment actual:

```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: api-gateway
  namespace: services
  labels:
    app: api-gateway
    version: v2.13.10
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: api-gateway
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1
      maxUnavailable: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: api-gateway
      annotations:
        prometheus.io/scrape: "true"
        prometheus.io/port: "6246"
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: api-gateway-sa
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 6976
        fsGroup: 47117
      containers:
        - name: api-gateway
          image: registry.example.com/api-gateway:v1.0.3
          ports:
            - containerPort: 6246
          env:
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: api-gateway-secrets
                  key: database_url
            - name: REDIS_URL
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: api-gateway-secrets
                  key: redis_url
            - name: LOG_LEVEL
              value: debug
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 1000m
              memory: 128Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 2000m
              memory: 256Mi
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: 6246
            initialDelaySeconds: 12
            periodSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 3
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /ready
              port: 6246
            initialDelaySeconds: 7
            periodSeconds: 5
            failureThreshold: 3
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /app/config
              readOnly: true
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: api-gateway-config
      topologySpreadConstraints:
        - maxSkew: 1
          topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname
          whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule
          labelSelector:
            matchLabels:
              app: api-gateway
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: api-gateway-pdb
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: api-gateway
---
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: api-gateway-hpa
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: api-gateway
  minReplicas: 2
  maxReplicas: 12
  metrics:
    - type: Resource
      resource:
        name: cpu
        target:
          type: Utilization
          averageUtilization: 78
    - type: Resource
      resource:
        name: memory
        target:
          type: Utilization
          averageUtilization: 77
```

¿Cómo configuro multi-region en DynamoDB?

Necesito implementar multi-tenancy en nuestra plataforma SaaS que corre
en Kubernetes. Tenemos 450 clientes con requisitos de aislamiento
diferentes: algunos aceptan shared pods, otros requieren namespaces dedicados,
y 7 clientes enterprise necesitan clusters dedicados.
¿Cómo modelo esto en Kubernetes? ¿Uso un cluster por tenant, namespace por
tenant o algo intermedio? ¿Cómo gestiono el networking isolation con
NetworkPolicies? ¿Cómo escala esto a 1485 tenants?
¿Qué es un Ingress Controller y cuáles son los más usados?
¿Cuál es la diferencia entre ReplicaSet y Deployment?

Necesito una revisión completa de nuestro pipeline de CI/CD para el servicio data-pipeline
escrito en TypeScript. Llevamos 9 meses con esta configuración y queremos
mejorar: tiempo de pipeline actual 45 minutos, queremos bajar a 11 minutos.

Aquí está el workflow completo de GitHub Actions:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline — data-pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths:
      - 'services/data-pipeline/**'
      - '.github/workflows/data-pipeline.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'services/data-pipeline/**'

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: myorg/data-pipeline
  CLUSTER_NAME: prod-eks-3
  AWS_REGION: ap-southeast-1

jobs:
  lint-and-test:
    name: Lint & Unit Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up TypeScript
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          npm run lint

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: |
          npm run test:unit -- --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/data-pipeline'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/typescript
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: services/data-pipeline
          format: spdx-json

  build-and-push:
    name: Build & Push Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/data-pipeline
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ needs.build-and-push.outputs.image-digest }}
          format: 'sarif'
          exit-code:

Implementamos IRSA para seguridad en nuestro cluster de Kubernetes
pero seguimos teniendo findings en nuestros escaneos de seguridad con Snyk.
Los principales problemas son:
- 21 imágenes con CVEs críticos en producción
- Pods corriendo como root
- Capabilities no necesarias habilitadas
- Secrets con acceso excesivo
¿Cuál es el orden correcto para atacar estos problemas sin romper producción?
¿Cómo implemento Pod Security Standards de forma gradual?
¿Cómo veo el consumo de recursos por namespace en Kubernetes?
¿Diferencia entre ECS y EKS en AWS?
¿Cuándo usar SQS vs Event Hub?

Configuramos Flux CD pero el equipo de seguridad nos pide implementar
supply chain security completa. Necesitamos:
- Firma de imágenes (cosign/sigstore)
- SBOM generación y verificación
- Análisis de vulnerabilidades antes del deploy
- Policy enforcement en el cluster
¿Cómo integro esto en el pipeline sin que tarde más de 4 minutos?
¿Qué política de severidades bloqueantes vs warnings recomiendas para empezar?
¿Cómo veo el consumo de recursos por namespace en Kubernetes?
¿Cómo funciona el health check de Cloud Load Balancing?
¿Cómo hago debug de un pod en estado Pending en Kubernetes?

Tenemos un cluster de AKS en sa-east-1 con
5 nodos m5.xlarge. En las últimas semanas notamos que durante
el horario pico (12:00-17:00 UTC) el cluster llega al
80% de CPU y algunos pods quedan en Pending porque no hay recursos.
Actualmente tenemos cluster-autoscaler configurado con min=5,
max=15. El scale-up tarda unos 4 minutos lo que es
demasiado para nuestra SLA.
¿Cómo puedo reducir el tiempo de scale-up? ¿Vale la pena migrar a Karpenter?
¿Debería usar Overprovisioning? Explícame las opciones con sus trade-offs.

¿Cómo hago que un pod solo corra en nodos con GPU en Kubernetes?

```hcl
terraform {

}

}

Migramos de una base de datos monolítica ClickHouse a Cosmos DB.
El esquema actual tiene 145 tablas, 31 GB de datos y
99 queries complejos con múltiples JOINs.
El mayor problema: 32 de esos queries tienen más de 3 JOINs y
algunos usan window functions. El equipo no tiene experiencia con bases de datos NoSQL.
¿Cómo modelo las relaciones en NoSQL? ¿Qué queries simplemente no pueden
migrar y qué alternativas tengo? ¿Cómo hago la migración con zero downtime?

Necesito una revisión completa de nuestro pipeline de CI/CD para el servicio reporting-engine
escrito en Python. Llevamos 19 meses con esta configuración y queremos
mejorar: tiempo de pipeline actual 51 minutos, queremos bajar a 15 minutos.

Aquí está el workflow completo de GitHub Actions:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline — reporting-engine

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths:
      - 'services/reporting-engine/**'
      - '.github/workflows/reporting-engine.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'services/reporting-engine/**'

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: myorg/reporting-engine
  CLUSTER_NAME: prod-eks-5
  AWS_REGION: sa-east-1

jobs:
  lint-and-test:
    name: Lint & Unit Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"
          cache: pip

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          ruff check . && mypy .

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: |
          pytest tests/unit/ -v --cov=. --cov-report=xml

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/reporting-engine'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/python
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |

Necesito una revisión completa de nuestro pipeline de CI/CD para el servicio event-processor
escrito en TypeScript. Llevamos 18 meses con esta configuración y queremos
mejorar: tiempo de pipeline actual 44 minutos, queremos bajar a 8 minutos.

Aquí está el workflow completo de GitHub Actions:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline — event-processor

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths:
      - 'services/event-processor/**'
      - '.github/workflows/event-processor.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'services/event-processor/**'

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: myorg/event-processor
  CLUSTER_NAME: prod-eks-2
  AWS_REGION: ap-northeast-1

jobs:
  lint-and-test:
    name: Lint & Unit Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up TypeScript
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          npm run lint

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: |
          npm run test:unit -- --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/event-processor'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/typescript
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: services/event-processor
          format: spdx-json

  build-and-push:
    name: Build & Push Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/event-processor
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ needs.build-and-push.outputs.image-digest }}
          format: 'sarif'

Necesito una revisión completa de nuestro pipeline de CI/CD para el servicio reporting-engine
escrito en Node.js. Llevamos 20 meses con esta configuración y queremos
mejorar: tiempo de pipeline actual 31 minutos, queremos bajar a 9 minutos.

Aquí está el workflow completo de GitHub Actions:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline — reporting-engine

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths:
      - 'services/reporting-engine/**'
      - '.github/workflows/reporting-engine.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'services/reporting-engine/**'

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: myorg/reporting-engine
  CLUSTER_NAME: prod-eks-3
  AWS_REGION: ap-southeast-1

jobs:
  lint-and-test:
    name: Lint & Unit Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          npm run lint

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: |
          npm run test:unit -- --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/reporting-engine'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/node.js
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: services/reporting-engine
          format: spdx-json

  build-and-push:
    name: Build & Push Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/reporting-engine
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ needs.build-and-push.outputs.image-digest }}
          format: 'sarif'

name: CI/CD Pipeline — reporting-engine

on:

    branches: [main]

env:

  CLUSTER_NAME: prod-eks-1

        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          npm run lint

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: |
          npm run test:unit -- --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/reporting-engine'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:

            p/java
            p/owasp-top-ten

        run: |
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0

          path: services/reporting-engine
          format: spdx-json

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/reporting-engine
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master

Tenemos problemas de performance en ClickHouse en producción. Las queries
más lentas tardan hasta 12 segundos y afectan a los usuarios.
Tenemos 132 transacciones/segundo en hora pico.
El DBA identificó que el problema principal es N+1 queries desde el ORM
(SQLAlchemy), falta de índices en
11 tablas, y una query de reporting que hace full table scan
sobre 19 millones de filas.
¿Cuál es el orden de optimización? ¿Cuándo tiene sentido agregar una
read replica? ¿Cómo implemento query result caching con ElastiCache?

¿Qué son los taints y tolerations en Kubernetes?
¿Cómo expongo un servicio interno de Kubernetes al exterior sin Ingress?

Actualmente tenemos cluster-autoscaler configurado con min=5,

¿Cómo puedo reducir el tiempo de scale-up? ¿Vale la pena migrar a Karpenter?

Necesito una revisión completa de nuestro pipeline de CI/CD para el servicio notification-hub
escrito en Rust. Llevamos 9 meses con esta configuración y queremos
mejorar: tiempo de pipeline actual 32 minutos, queremos bajar a 15 minutos.

Aquí está el workflow completo de GitHub Actions:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline — notification-hub

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths:
      - 'services/notification-hub/**'
      - '.github/workflows/notification-hub.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'services/notification-hub/**'

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: myorg/notification-hub
  CLUSTER_NAME: prod-eks-5
  AWS_REGION: ap-northeast-1

jobs:
  lint-and-test:
    name: Lint & Unit Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Rust
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          npm run lint

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: |
          npm run test:unit -- --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/notification-hub'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/rust
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: services/notification-hub
          format: spdx-json

  build-and-push:
    name: Build & Push Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/notification-hub
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ needs.build-and-push.outputs.image-digest }}
          format: 'sarif'

Requisitos:

**Funcionales:**
- 1486 clientes empresariales con SLA 99.77%
- Multi-tenancy con aislamiento de datos (PCI-DSS + SOC2 + GDPR)
- API REST + GraphQL + WebSockets en tiempo real
- ML inference con modelos ONNX (<132ms p99)
- Data warehouse para analytics de clientes (52 TB/mes)
- CI/CD con rollback automático < 105 segundos

**No funcionales:**
- Peak: 73k rpm, baseline 36k rpm
- Latencia API: p50 < 22ms, p99 < 374ms
- RPO: 7 minutos, RTO: 10 minutos
- Budget: $138773/mes

Tenemos problemas de performance en MongoDB en producción. Las queries
más lentas tardan hasta 26 segundos y afectan a los usuarios.

El DBA identificó que el problema principal es N+1 queries desde el ORM

Necesito una revisión completa de nuestro pipeline de CI/CD para el servicio api-platform
escrito en TypeScript. Llevamos 13 meses con esta configuración y queremos
mejorar: tiempo de pipeline actual 44 minutos, queremos bajar a 10 minutos.

Aquí está el workflow completo de GitHub Actions:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline — api-platform

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths:
      - 'services/api-platform/**'
      - '.github/workflows/api-platform.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'services/api-platform/**'

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: myorg/api-platform
  CLUSTER_NAME: prod-eks-2
  AWS_REGION: us-west-2

jobs:
  lint-and-test:
    name: Lint & Unit Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up TypeScript
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          npm run lint

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: |
          npm run test:unit -- --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/api-platform'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/typescript
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: services/api-platform
          format: spdx-json

  build-and-push:
    name: Build & Push Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/api-platform
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ needs.build-and-push.outputs.image-digest }}
          format: 'sarif'
          exit-code: '1'

Necesito una revisión completa de nuestro pipeline de CI/CD para el servicio ml-serving
escrito en Java. Llevamos 22 meses con esta configuración y queremos
mejorar: tiempo de pipeline actual 39 minutos, queremos bajar a 13 minutos.

Aquí está el workflow completo de GitHub Actions:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline — ml-serving

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths:
      - 'services/ml-serving/**'
      - '.github/workflows/ml-serving.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'services/ml-serving/**'

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: myorg/ml-serving
  CLUSTER_NAME: prod-eks-5
  AWS_REGION: ap-northeast-1

jobs:
  lint-and-test:
    name: Lint & Unit Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Java
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          npm run lint

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: |
          npm run test:unit -- --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/ml-serving'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/java
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: services/ml-serving
          format: spdx-json

  build-and-push:
    name: Build & Push Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/ml-serving
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ needs.build-and-push.outputs.image-digest }}
          format: 'sarif'
          exit-code: '1'
          severity: 'CRITICAL'

  deplo

    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/reporting-engine'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/rust
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: services/reporting-engine
          format: spdx-json

  build-and-push:
    name: Build & Push Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/reporting-engine
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ needs.build-and-push.outputs.image-digest }}
          format: 'sarif'

Aquí está el workflow completo de GitHub Actions:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline — ml-serving

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths:
      - 'services/ml-serving/**'
      - '.github/workflows/ml-serving.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'services/ml-serving/**'

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: myorg/ml-serving
  CLUSTER_NAME: prod-eks-5
  AWS_REGION: sa-east-1

jobs:
  lint-and-test:
    name: Lint & Unit Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Go
        uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: "1.22"
          cache: go

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          go mod download

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          golangci-lint run ./...

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: |
          go test ./... -race -coverprofile=coverage.out

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/ml-serving'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/go
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |
          govulncheck ./...

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: services/ml-serving
          format: spdx-json

  build-and-push:
    name: Build & Push Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/ml-serving
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ needs.build-and-push.outputs.image-digest }}
          format: 'sarif'

          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/notification-hub
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ needs.build-and-push.outputs.image-digest }}
          format: 'sarif'
          exit-

Tenemos problemas de performance en Cassandra en producción. Las queries
más lentas tardan hasta 23 segundos y afectan a los usuarios.
Tenemos 775 transacciones/segundo en hora pico.
El DBA identificó que el problema principal es N+1 queries desde el ORM
(GORM), falta de índices en
9 tablas, y una query de reporting que hace full table scan
sobre 238 millones de filas.

read replica? ¿Cómo implemento query result caching con Memorystore?

algunos usan window functions. El equipo no tiene experiencia con bases de datos NoSQL.

Necesito una revisión completa de nuestro pipeline de CI/CD para el servicio notification-hub
escrito en Python. Llevamos 20 meses con esta configuración y queremos
mejorar: tiempo de pipeline actual 29 minutos, queremos bajar a 14 minutos.

Aquí está el workflow completo de GitHub Actions:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline — notification-hub

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths:
      - 'services/notification-hub/**'
      - '.github/workflows/notification-hub.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'services/notification-hub/**'

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: myorg/notification-hub
  CLUSTER_NAME: prod-eks-5
  AWS_REGION: ap-northeast-1

jobs:
  lint-and-test:
    name: Lint & Unit Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"
          cache: pip

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          ruff check . && mypy .

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: |
          pytest tests/unit/ -v --cov=. --cov-report=xml

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/notification-hub'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/python
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |
          pip-audit --requirement requirements.txt

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: services/notification-hub
          format: spdx-json

  build-and-push:
    name: Build & Push Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/notification-hub
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ needs.b

más lentas tardan hasta 16 segundos y afectan a los usuarios.

Necesito una revisión completa de nuestro pipeline de CI/CD para el servicio auth-service
escrito en Go. Llevamos 22 meses con esta configuración y queremos
mejorar: tiempo de pipeline actual 27 minutos, queremos bajar a 9 minutos.

Aquí está el workflow completo de GitHub Actions:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline — auth-service

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths:
      - 'services/auth-service/**'
      - '.github/workflows/auth-service.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'services/auth-service/**'

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: myorg/auth-service
  CLUSTER_NAME: prod-eks-3
  AWS_REGION: ap-southeast-1

jobs:
  lint-and-test:
    name: Lint & Unit Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Go
        uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: "1.22"
          cache: go

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          go mod download

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          golangci-lint run ./...

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: |
          go test ./... -race -coverprofile=coverage.out

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/auth-service'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/go
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |
          govulncheck ./...

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: services/auth-service
          format: spdx-json

  build-and-push:
    name: Build & Push Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/auth-service
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ needs.build-and-push.outputs.image-digest }}
          format: 'sarif'
          exit-code: '1'

Timeline del incidente:
```
[2026-04-05T01:44:36Z] Error from server (Timeout): etcd leader changed
[2026-04-05T01:44:36Z] CrashLoopBackOff: back-off 3m restarting failed container

Arquitectura completa para una plataforma SaaS B2B en AWS.
Requisitos:

**Funcionales:**
- 8348 clientes empresariales con SLA 99.84%
- Multi-tenancy con aislamiento de datos (PCI-DSS + SOC2 + GDPR)
- API REST + GraphQL + WebSockets en tiempo real
- ML inference con modelos ONNX (<53ms p99)
- Data warehouse para analytics de clientes (30 TB/mes)
- CI/CD con rollback automático < 32 segundos

**No funcionales:**
- Peak: 365k rpm, baseline 12k rpm
- Latencia API: p50 < 59ms, p99 < 321ms
- RPO: 12 minutos, RTO: 18 minutos
- Budget: $93844/mes

Arquitectura completa para una plataforma SaaS B2B en Azure.
Requisitos:

**Funcionales:**
- 1358 clientes empresariales con SLA 99.74%
- Multi-tenancy con aislamiento de datos (PCI-DSS + SOC2 + GDPR)
- API REST + GraphQL + WebSockets en tiempo real
- ML inference con modelos PyTorch (<96ms p99)
- Data warehouse para analytics de clientes (98 TB/mes)
- CI/CD con rollback automático < 42 segundos

**No funcionales:**
- Peak: 356k rpm, baseline 15k rpm
- Latencia API: p50 < 89ms, p99 < 365ms
- RPO: 14 minutos, RTO: 15 minutos
- Budget: $108924/mes

terraform {

}

  version = "5.1.2"

  name = "event-processor-producción-vpc"
  cidr = "10.69.0.0/16"

  private_subnets = ["10.24.1.0/24", "10.136.2.0/24", "10.148.3.0/24"]

  enable_nat_gateway   = true
  single_nat_gateway   = false
  enable_dns_hostnames = true

  tags = {
    "kubernetes.io/cluster/event-processor-producción" = "shared"
  }
}

module "eks" {
  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/eks/aws"
  version = "20.0.0"

  cluster_name    = "event-processor-producción"
  cluster_version = "1.28"

  vpc_id     = module.vpc.vpc_id
  subnet_ids = module.vpc.private_subnets

  cluster_endpoint_public_access = true

      min_size       = 1
      max_size       = 14

        role = "general"

      max_size       = 23
      desired_size   = 4
      instance_types = ["m5.xlarge",

      capacity_type  = "SPOT"
    }
  }

  cluster_addons = {
    coredns = {
      most_recent = true
    }
    kube-proxy = {
      most_recent = true
    }
    vpc-cni = {
      most_recent = true
    }
    aws-ebs-csi-driver = {
      most_recent              = true
      service_account_role_arn = module.ebs_csi_irsa_role.iam_role_arn
    }
  }
}

Tenemos un cluster de GKE en ap-southeast-1 con
20 nodos m5.xlarge. En las últimas semanas notamos que durante
el horario pico (9:00-14:00 UTC) el cluster llega al
80% de CPU y algunos pods quedan en Pending porque no hay recursos.
Actualmente tenemos cluster-autoscaler configurado con min=4,
max=14. El scale-up tarda unos 3 minutos lo que es
demasiado para nuestra SLA.
¿Cómo puedo reducir el tiempo de scale-up? ¿Vale la pena migrar a Karpenter?
¿Debería usar Overprovisioning? Explícame las opciones con sus trade-offs.
¿Diferencia entre ConfigMap y Secret en Kubernetes?
¿Cómo configuro un bucket de Blob Storage como estático website?
¿Qué es un Service Mesh y para qué sirve?

Tenemos problemas de performance en PostgreSQL en producción. Las queries
más lentas tardan hasta 13 segundos y afectan a los usuarios.
Tenemos 561 transacciones/segundo en hora pico.
El DBA identificó que el problema principal es N+1 queries desde el ORM
(Hibernate), falta de índices en
19 tablas, y una query de reporting que hace full table scan
sobre 301 millones de filas.
¿Cuál es el orden de optimización? ¿Cuándo tiene sentido agregar una
read replica? ¿Cómo implemento query result caching con Memorystore?
¿Diferencia entre ECS y EKS en AWS?
¿Cuál es la mejor práctica para gestionar secretos en Azure?
¿Cómo funciona el autoscaling en GKE?

Implementamos Trivy para seguridad en nuestro cluster de Kubernetes
pero seguimos teniendo findings en nuestros escaneos de seguridad con Falco.
Los principales problemas son:
- 26 imágenes con CVEs críticos en producción
- Pods corriendo como root
- Capabilities no necesarias habilitadas
- Secrets con acceso excesivo
¿Cuál es el orden correcto para atacar estos problemas sin romper producción?
¿Cómo implemento Pod Security Standards de forma gradual?

Tengo que reducir el costo de infraestructura en GCP en
un 29% sin degradar la disponibilidad. El gasto actual es ~$6437/mes.
Los principales costos son:
- Compute (52% del total): GKE nodes
- Storage (10%): Blob Storage y ClickHouse
- Transfer (15%): egress cross-region y CDN
- Managed services (10%): CodeBuild, WAF
¿Por dónde empiezo? ¿Qué herramienta uso para análisis de costos?
¿Cómo evalúo Reserved Instances vs Spot vs On-demand para cada workload?

Necesito una revisión completa de nuestro pipeline de CI/CD para el servicio auth-service
escrito en TypeScript. Llevamos 6 meses con esta configuración y queremos
mejorar: tiempo de pipeline actual 26 minutos, queremos bajar a 12 minutos.

Aquí está el workflow completo de GitHub Actions:

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline — auth-service

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths:
      - 'services/auth-service/**'
      - '.github/workflows/auth-service.yml'
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'services/auth-service/**'

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: myorg/auth-service
  CLUSTER_NAME: prod-eks-2
  AWS_REGION: ap-northeast-1

jobs:
  lint-and-test:
    name: Lint & Unit Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up TypeScript
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          npm ci

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          npm run lint

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: |
          npm run test:unit -- --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: lint-and-test
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/auth-service'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/typescript
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: services/auth-service
          format: spdx-json

  build-and-push:
    name: Build & Push Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/auth-service
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy on built image
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ needs.build-and-push.outputs.image-digest }}
          format: 'sarif'
          exit-code: '1'

```yaml
name: CI/CD Pipeline — auth-service

on:

        run: |
          go mod download

      - name: Run linter
        run: |
          golangci-lint run ./...

      - name: Run unit tests

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4

  security-scan:

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: 'services/auth-service'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'
          severity: 'CRITICAL,HIGH'
          exit-code: '1'

      - name: Run Semgrep
        uses: returntocorp/semgrep-action@v1
        with:
          config: >-
            p/security-audit
            p/go
            p/owasp-top-ten

      - name: Dependency audit
        run: |
          govulncheck ./...

      - name: Generate SBOM
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: services/auth-service
          format: spdx-json

  build-and-push:
    name: Build & Push Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [lint-and-test, security-scan]
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    outputs:
      image-digest: ${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}
      image-tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix={{branch}}-
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' }}

      - name: Build and push
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: services/auth-service
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          provenance: true
          sbom: true

      - name: Sign image with cosign
        env:
          COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL: 1
        run: |
          cosign sign --yes ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}@${{ steps.build.outputs.digest }}

  scan-image:
    name: Scan Container Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-push

      - name: Run Trivy on built image

Tenemos problemas de performance en ClickHouse en producción. Las queries
más lentas tardan hasta 28 segundos y afectan a los usuarios.
Tenemos 790 transacciones/segundo en hora pico.

(GORM), falta de índices en
16 tablas, y una query de reporting que hace full table scan
sobre 56 millones de filas.

read replica? ¿Cómo implemento query result caching con ElastiCache?

terraform {

  }

  default_tags {
    tags = {
      Environment = "desarrollo"
      Service     = "api-platform"
      ManagedBy   = "terraform"
    }
  }
}

module "vpc" {
  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/vpc/aws"
  version = "5.1.2"

  name = "api-platform-desarrollo-vpc"
  cidr = "10.0.0.0/16"

  azs             = ["ap-southeast-1a", "ap-southeast-1b", "ap-southeast-1c"]
  private_subnets = ["10.51.1.0/24", "10.160.2.0/24", "10.78.3.0/24"]
  public_subnets  = ["10.80.101.0/24", "10.147.102.0/24", "10.21.103.0/24"]

  enable_nat_gateway   = true
  single_nat_gateway   = true
  enable_dns_hostnames = true

  tags = {
    "kubernetes.io/cluster/api-platform-desarrollo" = "shared"
  }
}

module "eks" {
  source  = "terraform-aws-modules/eks/aws"
  version = "20.0.0"

  cluster_name    = "api-platform-desarrollo"
  cluster_version = "1.28"

  vpc_id     = module.vpc.vpc_id
  subnet_ids = module.vpc.private_subnets

  cluster_endpoint_public_access = true

  eks_managed_node_groups = {
    general = {
      min_size       = 1
      max_size       = 12
      desired_size   = 6
      instance_types = ["m5.xlarge"]

      labels = {
        role = "general"
      }
    }

    spot = {
      min_size       = 0
      max_size       = 29
      desired_size   = 7
      instance_types = ["m5.2xlarge",
                        "m4.2xlarge"]
      capacity_type  = "SPOT"
    }
  }

  cluster_addons = {
    coredns = {
      most_recent = true
    }
    kube-proxy = {
      most_recent = true
    }
    vpc-cni = {
      most_recent = true
    }
    aws-ebs-csi-driver = {
      most_recent              = true
      service_account_role_arn = module.ebs_csi_irsa_role.iam_role_arn
    }
  }
}
